cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Aboriginal Australian city where Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons, and Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path turn desert, stone desert, dry wadi, savanna, dry ground and sandstone, dark wood, bone, glass, canvas into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Warramirra should feel like a desert route-city where road, market, tax, terrace, and refuge are organized around remembered paths.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Warramirra organizes public life around memory as route and law: its districts convert Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Warramirra should be recognized by Sandstone, Dark Wood, Bone, Glass, Canvas, Desert, Stone Desert, Dry Wadi, Savanna, Dry Ground, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Dream Binding, Threshold Oaths, Archive Echoes, and the named anchors Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path, Caravanserai Beacon, and Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to Aboriginal Australian; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path, Caravanserai Beacon, Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, and the five section landmark families symbolize memory as route and law while warning against trade pressure distorting old paths.

canonLocks: Warramirra is a Aboriginal Australian city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path.
The Seat of Power is Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Transit / Caravan / Rail) preserves these exact anchor names: Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook.
Section 2 (Grand Market) preserves these exact anchor names: Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook.
Section 3 (Treasury / Taxation) preserves these exact anchor names: Treasury / Taxation Hall, Assay Hall, Treasury Taxation Gold-Seal Monument, Taxation Arcade Nook.
Section 4 (Agricultural Terraces) preserves these exact anchor names: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.
Section 5 (Refugee Commons) preserves these exact anchor names: Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, Ration Arcade Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Warramirra as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Warramirra's environment should make Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Caravanserai Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons.

civilization.people: Warramirra's people are Aboriginal Australian; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Caravanserai Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Warramirra immediately identifiable through Sandstone, Dark Wood, Bone, Glass, Canvas, Desert, Stone Desert, Dry Wadi, Savanna, Dry Ground, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Dream Binding, Threshold Oaths, Archive Echoes, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Caravanserai Beacon, Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use White Limestone Civic Blocks, Lantern Lit, Civic Blue Glow, lantern lines, Civic Blue Illumination, district lanterns, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with desert, stone desert, dry wadi, savanna, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
specialist houses serving Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of memory as route and law made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Sandstone, Dark Wood, Bone, Glass, Canvas, Desert, Stone Desert, Dry Wadi, Savanna, Dry Ground, and the Aboriginal Australian visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons.
storyHooks: access to Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Caravanserai Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Warramirra.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

seatOfPower.name: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Central authority made visible and source-backed.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The District 13 authority complex with formal approaches and city-specific materials.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Seat of government, petitions, records, rulings, and command visibility.
storyHooks: a ruling contradicts a section custom
a sealed record changes public interpretation
D13 becomes a pressure point during crisis

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Transit / Caravan / Rail with anchors: Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook.
S2 must preserve Grand Market with anchors: Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook.
S3 must preserve Treasury / Taxation with anchors: Treasury / Taxation Hall, Assay Hall, Treasury Taxation Gold-Seal Monument, Taxation Arcade Nook.
S4 must preserve Agricultural Terraces with anchors: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.
S5 must preserve Refugee Commons with anchors: Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, Ration Arcade Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path as the skyline symbol, Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court at the central civic authority, and Caravanserai Beacon as the readable public signal.
Arrange the five section identities so Warramirra does not converge with other cities: Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Treasury / Taxation, Agricultural Terraces, Refugee Commons.

Use terrain cues (Desert, Stone Desert, Dry Wadi, Savanna, Dry Ground), material cues (Sandstone, Dark Wood, Bone, Glass, Canvas), and magic cues (Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Dream Binding, Threshold Oaths, Archive Echoes) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
